entries.intro: A digger turned up an old coin the other day. Probably not worth anything, but he was excited about it.
details: He was showing it to everyone at the tavern like he'd struck a vein. It was green and barely readable.
Someone told him it might be worth a few coppers to a collector. I doubt it, but he's already spent it in his head.
reactions: People find old junk all the time. Doesn't make it treasure.

entries.intro: I keep meaning to go poking around outside town. You hear stories about people finding things in the dirt out there.
details: A friend of mine found a brass buckle once: said it was old military issue. Sold it for a decent meal.
I'd settle for a nice stone or an arrowhead. Something to put on the shelf.
reactions: It's a nice thought, anyway. Beats working for a living.

statCheck.pass: After heavy rains the ground shifts and old things work their way up. That's when most finds happen: not from looking, just from timing.
statCheck.fail: I wouldn't know where to start, honestly. I'd probably just find rocks.

entries.intro: Somebody's kid found a ring out by the old path. The whole family's convinced it's valuable.
details: It's just a band with some scratches on it. Could be anyone's: probably fell off a finger years ago.
The kid won't take it off now. Wears it on a string around his neck like it's a family heirloom.
reactions: Let the boy have his fun. It's worth more as a story than whatever a merchant would give him.

entries.intro: An old-timer swears there's good stuff buried out past the hills. He's been saying that for years.
details: Every season he goes out with a shovel and comes back with sore arms and nothing to show for it.
His wife just sighs when he brings it up. I think she's given up trying to talk sense into him.
reactions: I feel bad for the man. The hope is the only thing keeping him going out there.

statCheck.pass: Old foundations do turn up in places like that sometimes. Doesn't mean there's anything worth having, though: mostly broken pottery.
statCheck.fail: I wouldn't know what's out there. I'm not much for digging.

entries.intro: A woman found a handful of clay beads in the weeds and traded them to a passing merchant. Got a fair price, too.
details: She said she nearly tossed them: thought they were pebbles at first. The merchant spotted the glaze on them.
Now half the neighborhood is out there sifting through the weeds hoping to find more.
reactions: Good for her. Not every lucky find turns into a week of people crawling around in the mud, but here we are.

statCheck.pass: Glazed beads like that usually mean there was a kiln nearby at some point. Could be more scattered around, but nothing that'd make you rich.
statCheck.fail: I don't know anything about beads. A find's a find, I suppose.
